circuit_id_name.description: The circuit_id_name of a DHCP relay agent filter object. This filter identifies the circuit between the remote host and the relay agent. For example, the identifier can be the ingress interface number of the circuit access unit, perhaps concatenated with the unit ID number and slot number. Also, the circuit ID can be an ATM virtual circuit ID or cable data virtual circuit ID.

comment.description: A descriptive comment of a DHCP relay agent filter object.

extattrs.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object. For valid values for extensible attributes, see {extattrs:values}.

extattrs_all.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object, including default attributes.

is_circuit_id.description: The circuit ID matching rule of a DHCP relay agent filter object. The circuit_id value takes effect only if the value is "MATCHES_VALUE".

is_circuit_id_substring.description: Determines if the substring of circuit ID, instead of the full circuit ID, is matched.

is_remote_id.description: The remote ID matching rule of a DHCP relay agent filter object. The remote_id value takes effect only if the value is Matches_Value.

is_remote_id_substring.description: Determines if the substring of remote ID, instead of the full remote ID, is matched.

name.description: The name of a DHCP relay agent filter object.

remote_id_name.description: The remote ID name attribute of a relay agent filter object. This filter identifies the remote host. The remote ID name can represent many different things such as the caller ID telephone number for a dial-up connection, a user name for logging in to the ISP, a modem ID, etc. When the remote ID name is defined on the relay agent, the DHCP server will have a trusted relationship to identify the remote host. The remote ID name is considered as a trusted identifier.

agent_remote_id.description: The agent remote ID for the fixed address.

allow_telnet.description: This field controls whether the credential is used for both the Telnet and SSH credentials. If set to False, the credential is used only for SSH.

always_update_dns.description: This field controls whether only the DHCP server is allowed to update DNS, regardless of the DHCP client requests.

bootfile.description: The bootfile name for the fixed address. You can configure the DHCP server to support clients that use the boot file name option in their DHCPREQUEST messages.

bootserver.description: The bootserver address for the fixed address. You can specify the name and/or IP address of the boot server that the host needs to boot. The boot server IPv4 Address or name in FQDN format.

comment.description: The comment for the credential.

password.description: The CLI password.

cli_credentials.description: The CLI credentials for the fixed address.

client_identifier_prepend_zero.description: This field controls whether there is a prepend for the dhcp-client-identifier of a fixed address.

authority_type.description: Type of authority over the object.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address of the Grid Member.

ipv6addr.description: The IPv6 Address of the Grid Member.

name.description: The Grid member name

delegated_member.description: The Cloud Platform Appliance to which authority of the object is delegated.

delegated_root.description: Indicates the root of the delegation if delegated_scope is SUBTREE or RECLAIMING. This is not set otherwise.

delegated_scope.description: Indicates the scope of delegation for the object. This can be one of the following: NONE (outside any delegation), ROOT (the delegation point), SUBTREE (within the scope of a delegation), RECLAIMING (within the scope of a delegation being reclaimed, either as the delegation point or in the subtree).

mgmt_platform.description: Indicates the specified cloud management platform.

owned_by_adaptor.description: Determines whether the object was created by the cloud adapter or not.

tenant.description: Reference to the tenant object associated with the object, if any.

usage.description: Indicates the cloud origin of the object.

cloud_info.description: Structure containing all cloud API related information for this object.

comment.description: Comment for the fixed address; maximum 256 characters.

ddns_domainname.description: The dynamic DNS domain name the appliance uses specifically for DDNS updates for this fixed address.

ddns_hostname.description: The DDNS host name for this fixed address.

deny_bootp.description: If set to true, BOOTP settings are disabled and BOOTP requests will be denied.

device_location.description: The location of the device.

device_type.description: The type of the device.

device_vendor.description: The vendor of the device.

dhcp_client_identifier.description: The DHCP client ID for the fixed address.

disable.description: Determines whether a fixed address is disabled or not. When this is set to False, the fixed address is enabled.

disable_discovery.description: Determines if the discovery for this fixed address is disabled or not. False means that the discovery is enabled.

discover_now_status.description: The discovery status of this fixed address.

ap_ip_address.description: Discovered IP address of Wireless Access Point.

ap_name.description: Discovered name of Wireless Access Point.

ap_ssid.description: Service set identifier (SSID) associated with Wireless Access Point.

cisco_ise_endpoint_profile.description: The Endpoint Profile created in Cisco ISE.

cisco_ise_security_group.description: The Cisco ISE security group name.

cisco_ise_session_state.description: The Cisco ISE connection session state.

cmp_type.description: If the IP is coming from a Cloud environment, the Cloud Management Platform type.

device_contact.description: Contact information from device on which the IP address was discovered.

device_location.description: Location of device on which the IP address was discovered.

device_model.description: The model name of the end device in the vendor terminology.

device_port_name.description: The system name of the interface associated with the discovered IP address.

device_port_type.description: The hardware type of the interface associated with the discovered IP address.

device_type.description: The type of end host in vendor terminology.

device_vendor.description: The vendor name of the end host.

discovered_name.description: The name of the network device associated with the discovered IP address.

discoverer.description: Specifies whether the IP address was discovered by a NetMRI or NIOS discovery process.

duid.description: For IPv6 address only. The DHCP unique identifier of the discovered host. This is an optional field, and data might not be included.

endpoint_groups.description: A comma-separated list of the discovered endpoint groups.

first_discovered.description: The date and time the IP address was first discovered in Epoch seconds format.

iprg_state.description: The status for the IP address within port redundant group.

last_discovered.description: The date and time the IP address was last discovered in Epoch seconds format.

mac_address.description: The discovered MAC address for the host. This is the unique identifier of a network device. The discovery acquires the MAC address for hosts that are located on the same network as the Grid member that is running the discovery. This can also be the MAC address of a virtual entity on a specified vSphere server.

mgmt_ip_address.description: The management IP address of the end host that has more than one IP.

netbios_name.description: The name returned in the NetBIOS reply or the name you manually register for the discovered host.

network_component_contact.description: Contact information from the network component on which the IP address was discovered.

network_component_description.description: A textual description of the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_ip.description: The IPv4 Address or IPv6 Address of the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_location.description: Location of the network component on which the IP address was discovered.

network_component_model.description: Model name of the switch port connected to the end host in vendor terminology.

network_component_name.description: If a reverse lookup was successful for the IP address associated with this switch, the host name is displayed in this field.

network_component_port_description.description: A textual description of the switch port that is connected to the end device.

network_component_port_name.description: The name of the switch port connected to the end device.

network_component_port_number.description: The number of the switch port connected to the end device.

network_component_type.description: Identifies the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_vendor.description: The vendor name of the switch port connected to the end host.

open_ports.description: The list of opened ports on the IP address, represented as: "TCP: 21,22,23 UDP: 137,139". Limited to max total 1000 ports.

os.description: The operating system of the detected host or virtual entity. The OS can be one of the following: * Microsoft for all discovered hosts that have a non-null value in the MAC addresses using the NetBIOS discovery method. * A value that a TCP discovery returns. * The OS of a virtual entity on a vSphere server.

port_duplex.description: The negotiated or operational duplex setting of the switch port connected to the end device.

port_link_status.description: The link status of the switch port connected to the end device. Indicates whether it is connected.

port_speed.description: The interface speed, in Mbps, of the switch port.

port_status.description: The operational status of the switch port. Indicates whether the port is up or down.

port_vlan_description.description: The description of the VLAN of the switch port that is connected to the end device.

port_vlan_name.description: The name of the VLAN of the switch port.

port_vlan_number.description: The ID of the VLAN of the switch port.

task_name.description: The name of the discovery task.

tenant.description: Discovered tenant.

v_adapter.description: The name of the physical network adapter through which the virtual entity is connected to the appliance.

v_cluster.description: The name of the VMware cluster to which the virtual entity belongs.

v_datacenter.description: The name of the vSphere datacenter or container to which the virtual entity belongs.

v_entity_name.description: The name of the virtual entity.

v_entity_type.description: The virtual entity type. This can be blank or one of the following: Virtual Machine, Virtual Host, or Virtual Center. Virtual Center represents a VMware vCenter server.

v_host.description: The name of the VMware server on which the virtual entity was discovered.

v_switch.description: The name of the switch to which the virtual entity is connected.

vlan_port_group.description: Port group which the virtual machine belongs to.

vmhost_ip_address.description: IP address of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_mac_address.description: MAC address of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_name.description: Name of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_nic_names.description: List of all physical port names used by the virtual switch on the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted. Represented as: "eth1,eth2,eth3".

vmhost_subnet_cidr.description: CIDR subnet of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmi_is_public_address.description: Indicates whether the IP address is a public address.

vmi_private_address.description: Private IP address of the virtual machine.

vmi_tenant_id.description: ID of the tenant which virtual machine belongs to.

vport_conf_mode.description: Configured mode of the network adapter on the virtual switch where the virtual machine connected to.

vport_conf_speed.description: Configured speed of the network adapter on the virtual switch where the virtual machine connected to. Unit is kb.

vport_link_status.description: Link status of the network adapter on the virtual switch where the virtual machine connected to.

vport_mac_address.description: MAC address of the network adapter on the virtual switch where the virtual machine connected to.

vport_mode.description: Actual mode of the network adapter on the virtual switch where the virtual machine connected to.

vport_name.description: Name of the network adapter on the virtual switch connected with the virtual machine.

vport_speed.description: Actual speed of the network adapter on the virtual switch where the virtual machine connected to. Unit is kb.

vswitch_available_ports_count.description: Numer of available ports reported by the virtual switch on which the virtual machine/vport connected to.

vswitch_ipv6_enabled.description: Indicates the virtual switch has IPV6 enabled.

vswitch_segment_id.description: ID of the network segment on which the current virtual machine/vport connected to.

vswitch_segment_name.description: Name of the network segment on which the current virtual machine/vport connected to.

vswitch_segment_port_group.description: Port group of the network segment on which the current virtual machine/vport connected to.

vswitch_segment_type.description: Type of the network segment on which the current virtual machine/vport connected to.

vswitch_tep_dhcp_server.description: DHCP server of the virtual tunnel endpoint (VTEP) in the virtual switch.

vswitch_tep_ip.description: IP address of the virtual tunnel endpoint (VTEP) in the virtual switch.

vswitch_tep_multicast.description: Muticast address of the virtual tunnel endpoint (VTEP) in the virtual swtich.

vswitch_tep_port_group.description: Port group of the virtual tunnel endpoint (VTEP) in the virtual switch.

vswitch_tep_type.description: Type of virtual tunnel endpoint (VTEP) in the virtual switch.

vswitch_tep_vlan.description: VLAN of the virtual tunnel endpoint (VTEP) in the virtual switch.

vswitch_type.description: Type of the virtual switch: standard or distributed.

discovered_data.description: The discovered data for this fixed address.

enable_ddns.description: The dynamic DNS updates flag of a DHCP Fixed Address object. If set to True, the DHCP server sends DDNS updates to DNS servers in the same Grid, and to external DNS servers.

enable_immediate_discovery.description: Determines if the discovery for the fixed address should be immediately enabled.

enable_pxe_lease_time.description: Set this to True if you want the DHCP server to use a different lease time for PXE clients.

extattrs.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object.

extattrs_all.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object. For valid values for extensible attributes, see {extattrs:values}.

result_field.description: The result field of the function.

func_call.description: Specifies the function call to execute. The `next_available_ip` function is supported for Fixed Address.

ignore_dhcp_option_list_request.description: If this field is set to False, the appliance returns all DHCP options the client is eligible to receive, rather than only the list of options the client has requested.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 address for the Fixed Address. This field is `required` unless a `func_call` is specified to invoke `next_available_ip`.

is_invalid_mac.description: This flag reflects whether the MAC address for this fixed address is invalid.

type.description: The filter type. Valid values are: * MAC * NAC * Option

logic_filter_rules.description: This field contains the logic filters to be applied on the this fixed address. This list corresponds to the match rules that are written to the dhcpd configuration file.

mac.description: The MAC address value for this fixed address.

match_client.description: The match_client value for this fixed address. Valid values are: "MAC_ADDRESS": The fixed IP address is leased to the matching MAC address. "CLIENT_ID": The fixed IP address is leased to the matching DHCP client identifier. "RESERVED": The fixed IP address is reserved for later use with a MAC address that only has zeros. "CIRCUIT_ID": The fixed IP address is leased to the DHCP client with a matching circuit ID. Note that the "agent_circuit_id" field must be set in this case. "REMOTE_ID": The fixed IP address is leased to the DHCP client with a matching remote ID. Note that the "agent_remote_id" field must be set in this case.

ms_ad_user_data.description: The Microsoft Active Directory user related information.

name.description: The name of the DHCP option.

num.description: The code of the DHCP option.

type.description: The DHCP option type. Valid values are: * "16-bit signed integer" * "16-bit unsigned integer" * "32-bit signed integer" * "32-bit unsigned integer" * "64-bit unsigned integer" * "8-bit signed integer" * "8-bit unsigned integer (1,2,4,8)" * "8-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 16-bit integer" * "array of 16-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 32-bit integer" * "array of 32-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 64-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 8-bit integer" * "array of 8-bit unsigned integer" * "array of ip-address pair" * "array of ip-address" * "array of string" * "binary" * "boolean array of ip-address" * "boolean" * "boolean-text" * "domain-list" * "domain-name" * "encapsulated" * "ip-address" * "string" * "text"

user_class.description: The name of the user class with which this DHCP option is associated.

value.description: Value of the DHCP option. Required to be set for all options.

vendor_class.description: The name of the vendor class with which this DHCP option is associated.

ms_options.description: This field contains the Microsoft DHCP options for this fixed address.

ms_server.description: The Microsoft server associated with this fixed address.

name.description: This field contains the name of this fixed address.

network.description: The network to which this fixed address belongs, in IPv4 Address/CIDR format.

network_view.description: The name of the network view in which this fixed address resides.

nextserver.description: The name in FQDN and/or IPv4 Address format of the next server that the host needs to boot.

options.description: An array of DHCP option dhcpoption structs that lists the DHCP options associated with the object.

pxe_lease_time.description: The PXE lease time value for a DHCP Fixed Address object. Some hosts use PXE (Preboot Execution Environment) to boot remotely from a server. To better manage your IP resources, set a different lease time for PXE boot requests. You can configure the DHCP server to allocate an IP address with a shorter lease time to hosts that send PXE boot requests, so IP addresses are not leased longer than necessary. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the update is cached. Zero indicates that the update is not cached.

reserved_interface.description: The ref to the reserved interface to which the device belongs.

restart_if_needed.description: Restarts the member service. The restart_if_needed flag can trigger a restart on DHCP services only when it is enabled on CP member.

authentication_password.description: Authentication password for the SNMPv3 user.

authentication_protocol.description: Authentication protocol for the SNMPv3 user.

comment.description: Comments for the SNMPv3 user.

privacy_password.description: Privacy password for the SNMPv3 user.

privacy_protocol.description: Privacy protocol for the SNMPv3 user.

snmp3_credential.description: The SNMPv3 credential for this fixed address.For SNMP3 Credentials to be applied to this fixed address,use_snmp3_credential and use_cli_credentials must be true.

comment.description: Comments for the SNMPv1 and SNMPv2 users.

credential_group.description: Group for the SNMPv1 and SNMPv2 credential.

snmp_credential.description: The SNMP credential for this fixed address. If set to true, the SNMP credential will override member-level settings..For SNMP Credentials to be applied to this fixed address,use_snmp_credential must be true.

template.description: If set on creation, the fixed address will be created according to the values specified in the named template.

use_cli_credentials.description: If set to true, the CLI credential will override member-level settings.

use_snmp3_credential.description: Determines if the SNMPv3 credential should be used for the fixed address.

use_snmp_credential.description: If set to true, the SNMP credential will override member-level settings.

extattrfilters.description: External Attribute Filters are used to return a more specific list of results by filtering on external attributes. If you specify multiple filters, the results returned will have only resources that match all the specified filters.

filters.description: Filter are used to return a more specific list of results. Filters can be used to match resources by specific attributes, e.g. name. If you specify multiple filters, the results returned will have only resources that match all the specified filters.

max_results.description: Maximum number of objects to be returned. Defaults to 1000.

paging.description: Enable (1) or disable (0) paging for the data source query. When enabled, the system retrieves results in pages, allowing efficient handling of large result sets. Paging is enabled by default.

bootfile.description: The boot file name for the fixed address. You can configure the DHCP server to support clients that use the boot file name option in their DHCPREQUEST messages.

bootserver.description: The boot server address for the fixed address. You can specify the name and/or IP address of the boot server that the host needs to boot. The boot server IPv4 Address or name in FQDN format.

comment.description: A descriptive comment of a fixed address template object.

ddns_domainname.description: The dynamic DNS domain name the appliance uses specifically for DDNS updates for this fixed address.

ddns_hostname.description: The DDNS host name for this fixed address.

deny_bootp.description: Determines if BOOTP settings are disabled and BOOTP requests will be denied.

enable_ddns.description: Determines if the DHCP server sends DDNS updates to DNS servers in the same Grid, and to external DNS servers.

enable_pxe_lease_time.description: Set this to True if you want the DHCP server to use a different lease time for PXE clients.

extattrs.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object. For valid values for extensible attributes, see {extattrs:values}.

extattrs_all.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object, including default attributes.

ignore_dhcp_option_list_request.description: If this field is set to False, the appliance returns all DHCP options the client is eligible to receive, rather than only the list of options the client has requested.

type.description: The filter type. Valid values are: * MAC * NAC * Option

logic_filter_rules.description: This field contains the logic filters to be applied on this fixed address. This list corresponds to the match rules that are written to the dhcpd configuration file.

name.description: The name of a fixed address template object.

nextserver.description: The name in FQDN and/or IPv4 Address format of the next server that the host needs to boot.

number_of_addresses.description: The number of addresses for this fixed address.

offset.description: The start address offset for this fixed address.

name.description: Name of the DHCP option.

num.description: The code of the DHCP option.

You can configure the DHCP server to allocate an IP address with a shorter lease time to hosts that send PXE boot requests, so IP addresses are not leased longer than necessary. The cloud_api_compatible attribute must be set to true if any extensible attributes, such as the Terraform Internal ID, require cloud access; otherwise, it must be set to false.

number_of_addresses.description: The number of addresses in the DHCP exclusion range template.

offset.description: The address offset of the DHCP exclusion range template.

exclude.description: These are ranges of IPv6 addresses that the appliance does not use to assign to clients. You can use these excluded addresses as static IPv6 addresses. They contain the start and end addresses of the excluded range, and optionally, information about this excluded range.

ipv6addr.description: The IPv6 Address of the Grid Member.

name.description: The Grid member name

member.description: The member that will provide service for the IPv6 DHCP range. Set `server_association_type` to `MEMBER` if you want the server specified here to serve the range. For searching by this field, use an HTTP method that contains a body (POST or PUT) with MS DHCP server structure and the request should have option _method=GET.

name.description: Name of the IPv6 DHCP range template.

number_of_addresses.description: The number of addresses for the IPv6 DHCP range.

offset.description: The start address offset for the IPv6 DHCP range.

filter.description: The name of the DHCP filter.

permission.description: The permission to be applied.

option_filter_rules.description: This field contains the Option filters to be applied to this IPv6 range. The appliance uses the matching rules of these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

recycle_leases.description: Determines whether the leases are kept in Recycle Bin until one week after expiry. If this is set to False, the leases are permanently deleted.

server_association_type.description: The type of server that is going to serve the IPv6 DHCP range. Valid values are `MEMBER` and `NONE`.

address_type.description: The address type value for this IPv6 fixed address. When the address type is "ADDRESS", a value for the 'ipv6addr' member is required. When the address type is "PREFIX", values for 'ipv6prefix' and 'ipv6prefix_bits' are required. When the address type is "BOTH", values for 'ipv6addr', 'ipv6prefix', and 'ipv6prefix_bits' are all required.

allow_telnet.description: This field controls whether the credential is used for both the Telnet and SSH credentials. If set to False, the credential is used only for SSH.

comment.description: The comment for the credential.

password.description: The CLI password.

cli_credentials.description: The CLI credentials for the IPv6 fixed address.

authority_type.description: Type of authority over the object.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address of the Grid Member.

ipv6addr.description: The IPv6 Address of the Grid Member.

name.description: The Grid member name

delegated_root.description: Indicates the root of the delegation if delegated_scope is SUBTREE or RECLAIMING. This is not set otherwise.

delegated_scope.description: Indicates the scope of delegation for the object. This can be one of the following: NONE (outside any delegation), ROOT (the delegation point), SUBTREE (within the scope of a delegation), RECLAIMING (within the scope of a delegation being reclaimed, either as the delegation point or in the subtree).

mgmt_platform.description: Indicates the specified cloud management platform.

owned_by_adaptor.description: Determines whether the object was created by the cloud adapter or not.

tenant.description: Reference to the tenant object associated with the object, if any.

usage.description: Indicates the cloud origin of the object.

cloud_info.description: Structure containing all cloud API related information for this object.

comment.description: Comment for the fixed address; maximum 256 characters.

device_location.description: The location of the device.

device_type.description: The type of the device.

device_vendor.description: The vendor of the device.

disable.description: Determines whether a fixed address is disabled or not. When this is set to False, the IPv6 fixed address is enabled.

disable_discovery.description: Determines if the discovery for this IPv6 fixed address is disabled or not. False means that the discovery is enabled.

discover_now_status.description: The discovery status of this IPv6 fixed address.

ap_ip_address.description: Discovered IP address of Wireless Access Point.

ap_name.description: Discovered name of Wireless Access Point.

ap_ssid.description: Service set identifier (SSID) associated with Wireless Access Point.

cisco_ise_endpoint_profile.description: The Endpoint Profile created in Cisco ISE.

cisco_ise_security_group.description: The Cisco ISE security group name.

cisco_ise_session_state.description: The Cisco ISE connection session state.

cmp_type.description: If the IP is coming from a Cloud environment, the Cloud Management Platform type.

device_contact.description: Contact information from device on which the IP address was discovered.

device_location.description: Location of device on which the IP address was discovered.

device_model.description: The model name of the end device in the vendor terminology.

device_port_name.description: The system name of the interface associated with the discovered IP address.

device_port_type.description: The hardware type of the interface associated with the discovered IP address.

device_type.description: The type of end host in vendor terminology.

device_vendor.description: The vendor name of the end host.

discovered_name.description: The name of the network device associated with the discovered IP address.

discoverer.description: Specifies whether the IP address was discovered by a NetMRI or NIOS discovery process.

duid.description: For IPv6 address only. The DHCP unique identifier of the discovered host. This is an optional field, and data might not be included.

endpoint_groups.description: A comma-separated list of the discovered endpoint groups.

first_discovered.description: The date and time the IP address was first discovered in Epoch seconds format.

iprg_state.description: The status for the IP address within port redundant group.

last_discovered.description: The date and time the IP address was last discovered in Epoch seconds format.

mac_address.description: The discovered MAC address for the host. This is the unique identifier of a network device. The discovery acquires the MAC address for hosts that are located on the same network as the Grid member that is running the discovery. This can also be the MAC address of a virtual entity on a specified vSphere server.

mgmt_ip_address.description: The management IP address of the end host that has more than one IP.

netbios_name.description: The name returned in the NetBIOS reply or the name you manually register for the discovered host.

network_component_contact.description: Contact information from the network component on which the IP address was discovered.

network_component_description.description: A textual description of the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_ip.description: The IPv4 Address or IPv6 Address of the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_location.description: Location of the network component on which the IP address was discovered.

network_component_model.description: Model name of the switch port connected to the end host in vendor terminology.

network_component_name.description: If a reverse lookup was successful for the IP address associated with this switch, the host name is displayed in this field.

network_component_port_description.description: A textual description of the switch port that is connected to the end device.

network_component_port_name.description: The name of the switch port connected to the end device.

network_component_port_number.description: The number of the switch port connected to the end device.

network_component_type.description: Identifies the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_vendor.description: The vendor name of the switch port connected to the end host.

open_ports.description: The list of opened ports on the IP address, represented as: "TCP: 21,22,23 UDP: 137,139". Limited to max total 1000 ports.

os.description: The operating system of the detected host or virtual entity. The OS can be one of the following: * Microsoft for all discovered hosts that have a non-null value in the MAC addresses using the NetBIOS discovery method. * A value that a TCP discovery returns. * The OS of a virtual entity on a vSphere server.

port_duplex.description: The negotiated or operational duplex setting of the switch port connected to the end device.

port_link_status.description: The link status of the switch port connected to the end device. Indicates whether it is connected.

port_speed.description: The interface speed, in Mbps, of the switch port.

port_status.description: The operational status of the switch port. Indicates whether the port is up or down.

port_vlan_description.description: The description of the VLAN of the switch port that is connected to the end device.

port_vlan_name.description: The name of the VLAN of the switch port.

port_vlan_number.description: The ID of the VLAN of the switch port.

task_name.description: The name of the discovery task.

tenant.description: Discovered tenant.

v_adapter.description: The name of the physical network adapter through which the virtual entity is connected to the appliance.

v_cluster.description: The name of the VMware cluster to which the virtual entity belongs.

v_datacenter.description: The name of the vSphere datacenter or container to which the virtual entity belongs.

v_entity_name.description: The name of the virtual entity.

v_entity_type.description: The virtual entity type. This can be blank or one of the following: Virtual Machine, Virtual Host, or Virtual Center. Virtual Center represents a VMware vCenter server.

v_host.description: The name of the VMware server on which the virtual entity was discovered.

v_switch.description: The name of the switch to which the virtual entity is connected.

vlan_port_group.description: Port group which the virtual machine belongs to.

vmhost_ip_address.description: IP address of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_mac_address.description: MAC address of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_name.description: Name of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_nic_names.description: List of all physical port names used by the virtual switch on the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted. Represented as: "eth1,eth2,eth3".

vmhost_subnet_cidr.description: CIDR subnet of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmi_is_public_address.description: Indicates whether the IP address is a public address.

vmi_private_address.description: Private IP address of the virtual machine.

vmi_tenant_id.description: ID of the tenant which virtual machine belongs to.

vswitch_ipv6_enabled.description: Indicates the virtual switch has IPV6 enabled.

When the address type is "PREFIX", values for 'ipv6_start_prefix', 'ipv6_end_prefix', and 'ipv6_prefix_bits' are required. permission.description: The permission to be applied.

fingerprint_filter_rules.description: This field contains the fingerprint filters for this DHCP range. The appliance uses matching rules in these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

high_water_mark.description: The percentage of DHCP range usage threshold above which range usage is not expected and may warrant your attention. When the high watermark is reached, the Infoblox appliance generates a syslog message and sends a warning (if enabled). A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100.

high_water_mark_reset.description: The percentage of DHCP range usage below which the corresponding SNMP trap is reset. A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100. The high watermark reset value must be lower than the high watermark value.

ignore_dhcp_option_list_request.description: If this field is set to False, the appliance returns all DHCP options the client is eligible to receive, rather than only the list of options the client has requested.

ignore_id.description: Indicates whether the appliance will ignore DHCP client IDs or MAC addresses. Valid values are "NONE", "CLIENT", or "MACADDR". The default is "NONE".

ignore_mac_addresses.description: A list of MAC addresses the appliance will ignore.

is_split_scope.description: This field will be 'true' if this particular range is part of a split scope.

known_clients.description: Permission for known clients. This can be 'Allow' or 'Deny'. If set to 'Deny' known clients will be denied IP addresses. Known clients include roaming hosts and clients with fixed addresses or DHCP host entries. Unknown clients include clients that are not roaming hosts and clients that do not have fixed addresses or DHCP host entries.

lease_scavenge_time.description: An integer that specifies the period of time (in seconds) that frees and backs up leases remained in the database before they are automatically deleted. To disable lease scavenging, set the parameter to -1. The minimum positive value must be greater than 86400 seconds (1 day).

type.description: The filter type. Valid values are: * MAC * NAC * Option

logic_filter_rules.description: This field contains the logic filters to be applied to this range. This list corresponds to the match rules that are written to the dhcpd configuration file.

low_water_mark.description: The percentage of DHCP range usage below which the Infoblox appliance generates a syslog message and sends a warning (if enabled). A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100.

low_water_mark_reset.description: The percentage of DHCP range usage threshold below which range usage is not expected and may warrant your attention. When the low watermark is crossed, the Infoblox appliance generates a syslog message and sends a warning (if enabled). A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100. The low watermark reset value must be higher than the low watermark value.

filter.description: The name of the DHCP filter.

permission.description: The permission to be applied.

mac_filter_rules.description: This field contains the MAC filters to be applied to this range. The appliance uses the matching rules of these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address of the Grid Member.

ipv6addr.description: The IPv6 Address of the Grid Member.

name.description: The Grid member name

member.description: This field contains the member that will run the DHCP service for this range. If this is not set, the range will be served by the member that is currently serving the network.

ms_ad_user_data.description: This field contains the Microsoft AD user data for this range. This data is used to create a user in the Microsoft AD when a lease is assigned to a host in this range.

name.description: The name of the DHCP option.

num.description: The code of the DHCP option.

type.description: The DHCP option type. Valid values are: * "16-bit signed integer" * "16-bit unsigned integer" * "32-bit signed integer" * "32-bit unsigned integer" * "64-bit unsigned integer" * "8-bit signed integer" * "8-bit unsigned integer (1,2,4,8)" * "8-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 16-bit integer" * "array of 16-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 32-bit integer" * "array of 32-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 64-bit unsigned integer" * "array of 8-bit integer" * "array of 8-bit unsigned integer" * "array of ip-address pair" * "array of ip-address" * "array of string" * "binary" * "boolean array of ip-address" * "boolean" * "boolean-text" * "domain-list" * "domain-name" * "encapsulated" * "ip-address" * "string" * "text"

user_class.description: The name of the user class with which this DHCP option is associated.

value.description: Value of the DHCP option. Required to be set for all options.

vendor_class.description: The name of the vendor class with which this DHCP option is associated.

ms_options.description: This field contains the Microsoft DHCP options for this range.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address or FQDN of the Microsoft server.

ms_server.description: This field contains the Microsoft server that will serve this range. This is used for Microsoft failover.

filter.description: The name of the DHCP filter.

permission.description: The permission to be applied.

nac_filter_rules.description: This field contains the NAC filters to be applied to this range. The appliance uses the matching rules of these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

name.description: This field contains the name of the Microsoft scope.

network.description: The network to which this range belongs, in IPv4 Address/CIDR format.

network_view.description: The name of the network view in which this range resides.

nextserver.description: The name in FQDN and/or IPv4 Address of the next server that the host needs to boot.

filter.description: The name of the DHCP filter.

permission.description: The permission to be applied.

option_filter_rules.description: This field contains the Option filters to be applied to this range. The appliance uses the matching rules of these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

name.description: Name of the DHCP option.

num.description: The code of the DHCP option.

use_option.description: Only applies to special options that are displayed separately from other options and have a use flag. These options are: * routers * router-templates * domain-name-servers * domain-name * broadcast-address * broadcast-address-offset * dhcp-lease-time * dhcp6.name-servers

value.description: Value of the DHCP option. Required to be set for all options.

vendor_class.description: The name of the space this DHCP option is associated to.

options.description: An array of DHCP option dhcpoption structs that lists the DHCP options associated with the object.

blackout_duration.description: The blackout duration in seconds; minimum value is 1 minute.

day_of_month.description: The day of the month for the scheduled task.

disable.description: If set to True, the scheduled task is disabled.

every.description: The number of frequency to wait before repeating the scheduled task.

frequency.description: The frequency for the scheduled task.

hour_of_day.description: The hour of day for the scheduled task.

minutes_past_hour.description: The minutes past the hour for the scheduled task.

month.description: The month for the scheduled task.

recurring_time.description: The recurring time for the schedule in Epoch seconds format. This field is obsolete and is preserved only for backward compatibility purposes. Please use other applicable fields to define the recurring schedule. DO NOT use recurring_time together with these fields. If you use recurring_time with other fields to define the recurring schedule, recurring_time has priority over year, hour_of_day, and minutes_past_hour and will override the values of these fields, although it does not override month and day_of_month. In this case, the recurring time value might be different than the intended value that you define.

repeat.description: Indicates if the scheduled task will be repeated or run only once.

time_zone.description: The time zone for the schedule.

weekdays.description: Days of the week when scheduling is triggered.

year.description: The year for the scheduled task.

blackout_schedule.description: The schedule for the blackout period.

enable_blackout.description: Determines whether a blackout is enabled or not.

port_control_blackout_setting.description: The port control blackout settings for the range. This field is used to configure the port control blackout settings for the DHCP range. It includes information about the blackout settings, such as the start and end times of the blackout period.

pxe_lease_time.description: The PXE lease time value of a DHCP Range object. Some hosts use PXE (Preboot Execution Environment) to boot remotely from a server. To better manage your IP resources, set a different lease time for PXE boot requests. You can configure the DHCP server to allocate an IP address with a shorter lease time to hosts that send PXE boot requests, so IP addresses are not leased longer than necessary. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the update is cached. Zero indicates that the update is not cached.

recycle_leases.description: If the field is set to True, the leases are kept in the Recycle Bin until one week after expiration. Otherwise, the leases are permanently deleted.

filter.description: The name of the DHCP filter.

permission.description: The permission to be applied.

relay_agent_filter_rules.description: This field contains the Relay Agent filters to be applied to this range. The appliance uses the matching rules of these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

same_port_control_discovery_blackout.description: If the field is set to True, the discovery blackout setting will be used for port control blackout setting.

server_association_type.description: The type of server that is going to serve the range.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address or FQDN of the Microsoft server.

split_member.description: This field contains the split member that will run the DHCP service for this range. If this is not set, the range will be served by the member that is currently serving the network.

split_scope_exclusion_percent.description: This field controls the percentage used when creating a split scope. Valid values are numbers between 1 and 99. If the value is 40, it means that the top 40% of the exclusion will be created on the DHCP range assigned to {next_available_ip:next_available_ip} and the lower 60% of the range will be assigned to DHCP range assigned to {next_available_ip:next_available_ip}

start_addr.description: The IPv4 Address starting address of the range.

static_hosts.description: The number of static DHCP addresses configured in the range.

enabled_attributes.description: The list of Cisco ISE attributes allowed for subscription.

mapped_ea.description: The name of the extensible attribute definition object the Cisco ISE attribute that is enabled for subscription is mapped on.

name.description: The Cisco ISE attribute name that is enabled for publishsing from a Cisco ISE endpoint.

mapped_ea_attributes.description: The list of NIOS extensible attributes to Cisco ISE attributes mappings.

subscribe_settings.description: The subscribe settings for the range. This field is used to configure the subscription settings for the DHCP range. It includes information about the subscription, such as the subscriber's email address and whether the subscription is enabled.

template.description: If set on creation, the range will be created according to the values specified in the named template.

total_hosts.description: The total number of DHCP addresses configured in the range.

unknown_clients.description: Permission for unknown clients. This can be 'Allow' or 'Deny'. If set to 'Deny', unknown clients will be denied IP addresses. Known clients include roaming hosts and clients with fixed addresses or DHCP host entries. Unknown clients include clients that are not roaming hosts and clients that do not have fixed addresses or DHCP host entries.

update_dns_on_lease_renewal.description: This field controls whether the DHCP server updates DNS when a DHCP lease is renewed.

use_blackout_setting.description: Use flag for: discovery_blackout_setting , port_control_blackout_setting, same_port_control_discovery_blackout

block.description: Retrieves information about existing DHCP Ranges.

extattrfilters.description: External Attribute Filters are used to return a more specific list of results by filtering on external attributes. If you specify multiple filters, the results returned will have only resources that match all the specified filters.

filters.description: Filter are used to return a more specific list of results. Filters can be used to match resources by specific attributes, e.g. name. If you specify multiple filters, the results returned will have only resources that match all the specified filters.

max_results.description: Maximum number of objects to be returned. Defaults to 1000.

paging.description: Enable (1) or disable (0) paging for the data source query. When enabled, the system retrieves results in pages, allowing efficient handling of large result sets. Paging is enabled by default.

bootfile.description: The bootfile name for the range. You can configure the DHCP server to support clients that use the boot file name option in their DHCPREQUEST messages.

bootserver.description: The bootserver address for the range. You can specify the name and/or IP address of the boot server that the host needs to boot. The boot server IPv4 Address or name in FQDN format.

cloud_api_compatible.description: Determines whether the IPv6 DHCP range template can be used to create network objects in a cloud-computing deployment. The cloud_api_compatible attribute must be set to true if any extensible attributes, such as the Terraform Internal ID, require cloud access; otherwise, it must be set to false.

comment.description: A descriptive comment of a range template object.

ddns_domainname.description: The dynamic DNS domain name the appliance uses specifically for DDNS updates for this range.

ddns_generate_hostname.description: If this field is set to True, the DHCP server generates a hostname and updates DNS with it when the DHCP client request does not contain a hostname.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address of the Grid Member.

ipv6addr.description: The IPv6 Address of the Grid Member.

name.description: The Grid member name

delegated_member.description: The vconnector member that the object should be delegated to when created from this range template.

deny_all_clients.description: If True, send NAK forcing the client to take the new address.

deny_bootp.description: Determines if BOOTP settings are disabled and BOOTP requests will be denied.

email_list.description: The e-mail lists to which the appliance sends DHCP threshold alarm e-mail messages.

enable_ddns.description: Determines if the DHCP server sends DDNS updates to DNS servers in the same Grid, and to external DNS servers.

enable_dhcp_thresholds.description: Determines if DHCP thresholds are enabled for the range.

enable_email_warnings.description: Determines if DHCP threshold warnings are sent through email.

enable_pxe_lease_time.description: Set this to True if you want the DHCP server to use a different lease time for PXE clients.

enable_snmp_warnings.description: Determines if DHCP threshold warnings are sent through SNMP.

comment.description: A descriptive comment of a DHCP exclusion range template.

number_of_addresses.description: The number of addresses in the DHCP exclusion range template.

offset.description: The address offset of the DHCP exclusion range template.

exclude.description: These are ranges of IP addresses that the appliance does not use to assign to clients. You can use these exclusion addresses as static IP addresses. They contain the start and end addresses of the exclusion range, and optionally, information about this exclusion range.

extattrs.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object.

extattrs_all.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object , including default attributes.

failover_association.description: The name of the failover association: the server in this failover association will serve the IPv4 range in case the main server is out of service. {rangetemplate:rangetemplate} must be set to 'FAILOVER' or 'FAILOVER_MS' if you want the failover association specified here to serve the range.

filter.description: The name of the DHCP filter.

permission.description: The permission to be applied.

fingerprint_filter_rules.description: This field contains the fingerprint filters for this DHCP range. The appliance uses matching rules in these filters to select the address range from which it assigns a lease.

high_water_mark.description: The percentage of DHCP range usage threshold above which range usage is not expected and may warrant your attention. When the high watermark is reached, the Infoblox appliance generates a syslog message and sends a warning (if enabled). A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100.

high_water_mark_reset.description: The percentage of DHCP range usage below which the corresponding SNMP trap is reset. A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100. The high watermark reset value must be lower than the high watermark value.

ignore_dhcp_option_list_request.description: If this field is set to False, the appliance returns all DHCP options the client is eligible to receive, rather than only the list of options the client has requested.

known_clients.description: Permission for known clients. If set to 'Deny' known clients will be denied IP addresses. Known clients include roaming hosts and clients with fixed addresses or DHCP host entries. Unknown clients include clients that are not roaming hosts and clients that do not have fixed addresses or DHCP host entries.

To disable lease scavenging, set the parameter to -1. The minimum positive value must be greater than 86400 seconds (1 day).

When the low watermark is crossed, the Infoblox appliance generates a syslog message and sends a warning (if enabled). A number that specifies the percentage of allocated addresses. The range is from 1 to 100. The low watermark reset value must be higher than the low watermark value.

To better manage your IP resources, set a different lease time for PXE boot requests. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the update is cached. Zero indicates that the update is not cached.

address_type.description: The address type for this roaming host.

bootfile.description: The bootfile name for the roaming host. You can configure the DHCP server to support clients that use the boot file name option in their DHCPREQUEST messages.

bootserver.description: The boot server address for the roaming host. You can specify the name and/or IP address of the boot server that the host needs to boot. The boot server IPv4 Address or name in FQDN format.

client_identifier_prepend_zero.description: This field controls whether there is a prepend for the dhcp-client-identifier of a roaming host.

comment.description: Comment for the roaming host; maximum 256 characters.

ddns_domainname.description: The DDNS domain name for this roaming host.

ddns_hostname.description: The DDNS host name for this roaming host.

deny_bootp.description: If set to true, BOOTP settings are disabled and BOOTP requests will be denied.

dhcp_client_identifier.description: The DHCP client ID for the roaming host.

disable.description: Determines whether a roaming host is disabled or not. When this is set to False, the roaming host is enabled.

enable_ddns.description: The dynamic DNS updates flag of the roaming host object. If set to True, the DHCP server sends DDNS updates to DNS servers in the same Grid, and to external DNS servers.

enable_pxe_lease_time.description: Set this to True if you want the DHCP server to use a different lease time for PXE clients.

extattrs.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object.

extattrs_all.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object, including default and internal attributes.

force_roaming_hostname.description: Set this to True to use the roaming host name as its ddns_hostname.

ignore_dhcp_option_list_request.description: If this field is set to False, the appliance returns all the DHCP options the client is eligible to receive, rather than only the list of options the client has requested.

ipv6_client_hostname.description: The client hostname of a DHCP roaming host object. This field specifies the host name that the DHCP client sends to the Infoblox appliance using DHCP option 12.

ipv6_ddns_domainname.description: The IPv6 DDNS domain name for this roaming host.

ipv6_ddns_hostname.description: The IPv6 DDNS host name for this roaming host.

ipv6_domain_name.description: The IPv6 domain name for this roaming host.

ipv6_domain_name_servers.description: The IPv6 addresses of DNS recursive name servers to which the DHCP client can send name resolution requests. The DHCP server includes this information in the DNS Recursive Name Server option in Advertise, Rebind, Information-Request, and Reply messages.

ipv6_duid.description: The DUID value for this roaming host.

ipv6_enable_ddns.description: Set this to True to enable IPv6 DDNS.

ipv6_force_roaming_hostname.description: Set this to True to use the roaming host name as its ddns_hostname.

ipv6_mac_address.description: The MAC address for this roaming host.

ipv6_match_option.description: The identification method for an IPv6 or mixed IPv4/IPv6 roaming host. The supported values for this field are "DUID" or "V6_MAC_ADDRESS", which specify what option should be used to identify the specific DHCPv6 client.

name.description: Name of the DHCP option.

num.description: The code of the DHCP option.

vendor_class.description: The name of the space this DHCP option is associated to.

ipv6_options.description: An array of DHCP option dhcpoption structs that lists the DHCP options associated with the object.

ipv6_template.description: If set on creation, the roaming host will be created according to the values specified in the named IPv6 roaming host template.

mac.description: The MAC address for this roaming host.

match_client.description: The match-client value for this roaming host. Valid values are: "MAC_ADDRESS": The fixed IP address is leased to the matching MAC address. "CLIENT_ID": The fixed IP address is leased to the matching DHCP client identifier.

To better manage your IP resources, set a different lease time for PXE boot requests. You can configure the DHCP server to allocate an IP address with a shorter lease time to hosts that send PXE boot requests, so IP addresses are not leased longer than necessary. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the update is cached. Zero indicates that the update is not cached.

valid_lifetime.description: The valid lifetime value for this roaming host object.

extattrfilters.description: External Attribute Filters are used to return a more specific list of results by filtering on external attributes. If you specify multiple filters, the results returned will have only resources that match all the specified filters.

filters.description: Filter are used to return a more specific list of results. Filters can be used to match resources by specific attributes, e.g. name. If you specify multiple filters, the results returned will have only resources that match all the specified filters.

max_results.description: Maximum number of objects to be returned. Defaults to 1000.

paging.description: Enable (1) or disable (0) paging for the data source query. When enabled, the system retrieves results in pages, allowing efficient handling of large result sets. Paging is enabled by default.

bootfile.description: The bootfile name for the shared network. You can configure the DHCP server to support clients that use the boot file name option in their DHCPREQUEST messages.

bootserver.description: The bootserver address for the shared network. You can specify the name and/or IP address of the boot server that the host needs to boot. The boot server IPv4 Address or name in FQDN format.

comment.description: Comment for the shared network, maximum 256 characters.

ddns_generate_hostname.description: If this field is set to True, the DHCP server generates a hostname and updates DNS with it when the DHCP client request does not contain a hostname.

ddns_server_always_updates.description: This field controls whether only the DHCP server is allowed to update DNS, regardless of the DHCP clients requests. Note that changes for this field take effect only if ddns_use_option81 is True.

ddns_ttl.description: The DNS update Time to Live (TTL) value of a shared network object. The TTL is a 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the update is cached. Zero indicates that the update is not cached.

ddns_update_fixed_addresses.description: By default, the DHCP server does not update DNS when it allocates a fixed address to a client. You can configure the DHCP server to update the A and PTR records of a client with a fixed address. When this feature is enabled and the DHCP server adds A and PTR records for a fixed address, the DHCP server never discards the records.

ddns_use_option81.description: The support for DHCP Option 81 at the shared network level.

deny_bootp.description: If set to true, BOOTP settings are disabled and BOOTP requests will be denied.

dhcp_utilization.description: The percentage of the total DHCP utilization of the networks belonging to the shared network multiplied by 1000. This is the percentage of the total number of available IP addresses from all the networks belonging to the shared network versus the total number of all IP addresses in all of the networks in the shared network.

dhcp_utilization_status.description: A string describing the utilization level of the shared network.

disable.description: Determines whether a shared network is disabled or not. When this is set to False, the shared network is enabled.

dynamic_hosts.description: The total number of DHCP leases issued for the shared network.

enable_ddns.description: The dynamic DNS updates flag of a shared network object. If set to True, the DHCP server sends DDNS updates to DNS servers in the same Grid, and to external DNS servers.

enable_pxe_lease_time.description: Set this to True if you want the DHCP server to use a different lease time for PXE clients.

extattrs.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object.

extattrs_all.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object , including default attributes.

ignore_client_identifier.description: If set to true, the client identifier will be ignored.

ignore_dhcp_option_list_request.description: If this field is set to False, the appliance returns all DHCP options the client is eligible to receive, rather than only the list of options the client has requested.

ignore_id.description: Indicates whether the appliance will ignore DHCP client IDs or MAC addresses. Valid values are "NONE", "CLIENT", or "MACADDR". The default is "NONE".

ignore_mac_addresses.description: A list of MAC addresses the appliance will ignore.

lease_scavenge_time.description: An integer that specifies the period of time (in seconds) that frees and backs up leases remained in the database before they are automatically deleted. To disable lease scavenging, set the parameter to -1. The minimum positive value must be greater than 86400 seconds (1 day).

type.description: The filter type. Valid values are: * MAC * NAC * Option

logic_filter_rules.description: This field contains the logic filters to be applied on the this shared network. This list corresponds to the match rules that are written to the dhcpd configuration file.

ms_ad_user_data.description: The Microsoft Active Directory user related information.

name.description: The name of the IPv6 Shared Network.

network_view.description: The name of the network view in which this shared network resides.

networks.description: A list of networks belonging to the shared network. Each individual list item must be specified as an object containing a 'ref' parameter to a network reference, for example:: [{ "ref": "network/ZG5zLm5ldHdvcmskMTAuMwLvMTYvMA" }] if the reference of the wanted network is not known, it is possible to specify search parameters for the network instead in the following way:: [{ "ref": { 'network': '10.0.0.0/8' } }] note that in this case the search must match exactly one network for the assignment to be successful.

nextserver.description: The name in FQDN and/or IPv4 Address of the next server that the host needs to boot.

name.description: Name of the DHCP option.

num.description: The code of the DHCP option.

use_option.description: Only applies to special options that are displayed separately from other options and have a use flag. These options are: * routers * router-templates * domain-name-servers * domain-name * broadcast-address * broadcast-address-offset * dhcp-lease-time * dhcp6.name-servers

value.description: Value of the DHCP option. Required to be set for all options.

vendor_class.description: The name of the space this DHCP option is associated to.

options.description: An array of DHCP option dhcpoption structs that lists the DHCP options associated with the object.

pxe_lease_time.description: The PXE lease time value of a shared network object. Some hosts use PXE (Preboot Execution Environment) to boot remotely from a server. To better manage your IP resources, set a different lease time for PXE boot requests. You can configure the DHCP server to allocate an IP address with a shorter lease time to hosts that send PXE boot requests, so IP addresses are not leased longer than necessary. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the update is cached. Zero indicates that the update is not cached.

static_hosts.description: The number of static DHCP addresses configured in the shared network.

total_hosts.description: The total number of DHCP addresses configured in the shared network.

update_dns_on_lease_renewal.description: This field controls whether the DHCP server updates DNS when a DHCP lease is renewed.

recurring_time.description: The recurring time for the schedule in Epoch seconds format. This field is obsolete and is preserved only for backward compatibility purposes. Please use other applicable fields to define the recurring schedule. DO NOT use recurring_time together with these fields. If you use recurring_time with other fields to define the recurring schedule, recurring_time has priority over year, hour_of_day, and minutes_past_hour and will override the values of these fields, although it does not override month and day_of_month. In this case, the recurring time value might be different than the intended value that you define.

region.description: Amazon EC2 region where this resource record resides for latency routing.

set_identifier.description: An identifier that differentiates records with the same DNS name and type for weighted, latency, geolocation, and failover routing.

type.description: Type of Amazon Route 53 resource record.

weight.description: Value that determines the portion of traffic for this record in weighted routing. The range is from 0 to 255.

aws_rte53_record_info.description: The AWS Route53 record information associated with the record.

authority_type.description: Type of authority over the object.

ipv4addr.description: The IPv4 Address of the Grid Member.

ipv6addr.description: The IPv6 Address of the Grid Member.

name.description: The Grid member name

delegated_root.description: Indicates the root of the delegation if delegated_scope is SUBTREE or RECLAIMING. This is not set otherwise.

delegated_scope.description: Indicates the scope of delegation for the object. This can be one of the following: NONE (outside any delegation), ROOT (the delegation point), SUBTREE (within the scope of a delegation), RECLAIMING (within the scope of a delegation being reclaimed, either as the delegation point or in the subtree).

mgmt_platform.description: Indicates the specified cloud management platform.

owned_by_adaptor.description: Determines whether the object was created by the cloud adapter or not.

tenant.description: Reference to the tenant object associated with the object, if any.

usage.description: Indicates the cloud origin of the object.

cloud_info.description: The cloud information associated with the record.

comment.description: Comment for the record; maximum 256 characters.

creation_time.description: The time of the record creation in Epoch seconds format.

creator.description: The record creator.

ddns_principal.description: The GSS-TSIG principal that owns this record.

ddns_protected.description: Determines if the DDNS updates for this record are allowed or not.

disable.description: Determines if the record is disabled or not. False means that the record is enabled.

ap_ip_address.description: Discovered IP address of Wireless Access Point.

ap_name.description: Discovered name of Wireless Access Point.

ap_ssid.description: Service set identifier (SSID) associated with Wireless Access Point.

cisco_ise_endpoint_profile.description: The Endpoint Profile created in Cisco ISE.

cisco_ise_security_group.description: The Cisco ISE security group name.

cisco_ise_session_state.description: The Cisco ISE connection session state.

cmp_type.description: If the IP is coming from a Cloud environment, the Cloud Management Platform type.

device_contact.description: Contact information from device on which the IP address was discovered.

device_location.description: Location of device on which the IP address was discovered.

device_model.description: The model name of the end device in the vendor terminology.

device_port_name.description: The system name of the interface associated with the discovered IP address.

device_port_type.description: The hardware type of the interface associated with the discovered IP address.

device_type.description: The type of end host in vendor terminology.

device_vendor.description: The vendor name of the end host.

discovered_name.description: The name of the network device associated with the discovered IP address.

discoverer.description: Specifies whether the IP address was discovered by a NetMRI or NIOS discovery process.

duid.description: For IPv6 address only. The DHCP unique identifier of the discovered host. This is an optional field, and data might not be included.

endpoint_groups.description: A comma-separated list of the discovered endpoint groups.

first_discovered.description: The date and time the IP address was first discovered in Epoch seconds format.

iprg_state.description: The status for the IP address within port redundant group.

last_discovered.description: The date and time the IP address was last discovered in Epoch seconds format.

mac_address.description: The discovered MAC address for the host. This is the unique identifier of a network device. The discovery acquires the MAC address for hosts that are located on the same network as the Grid member that is running the discovery. This can also be the MAC address of a virtual entity on a specified vSphere server.

mgmt_ip_address.description: The management IP address of the end host that has more than one IP.

netbios_name.description: The name returned in the NetBIOS reply or the name you manually register for the discovered host.

network_component_contact.description: Contact information from the network component on which the IP address was discovered.

network_component_description.description: A textual description of the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_ip.description: The IPv4 Address or IPv6 Address of the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_location.description: Location of the network component on which the IP address was discovered.

network_component_model.description: Model name of the switch port connected to the end host in vendor terminology.

network_component_name.description: If a reverse lookup was successful for the IP address associated with this switch, the host name is displayed in this field.

network_component_port_description.description: A textual description of the switch port that is connected to the end device.

network_component_port_name.description: The name of the switch port connected to the end device.

network_component_port_number.description: The number of the switch port connected to the end device.

network_component_type.description: Identifies the switch that is connected to the end device.

network_component_vendor.description: The vendor name of the switch port connected to the end host.

open_ports.description: The list of opened ports on the IP address, represented as: "TCP: 21,22,23 UDP: 137,139". Limited to max total 1000 ports.

os.description: The operating system of the detected host or virtual entity. The OS can be one of the following: * Microsoft for all discovered hosts that have a non-null value in the MAC addresses using the NetBIOS discovery method. * A value that a TCP discovery returns. * The OS of a virtual entity on a vSphere server.

port_duplex.description: The negotiated or operational duplex setting of the switch port connected to the end device.

port_link_status.description: The link status of the switch port connected to the end device. Indicates whether it is connected.

port_speed.description: The interface speed, in Mbps, of the switch port.

port_status.description: The operational status of the switch port. Indicates whether the port is up or down.

port_vlan_description.description: The description of the VLAN of the switch port that is connected to the end device.

port_vlan_name.description: The name of the VLAN of the switch port.

port_vlan_number.description: The ID of the VLAN of the switch port.

task_name.description: The name of the discovery task.

tenant.description: Discovered tenant.

v_adapter.description: The name of the physical network adapter through which the virtual entity is connected to the appliance.

v_cluster.description: The name of the VMware cluster to which the virtual entity belongs.

v_datacenter.description: The name of the vSphere datacenter or container to which the virtual entity belongs.

v_entity_name.description: The name of the virtual entity.

v_entity_type.description: The virtual entity type. This can be blank or one of the following: Virtual Machine, Virtual Host, or Virtual Center. Virtual Center represents a VMware vCenter server.

v_host.description: The name of the VMware server on which the virtual entity was discovered.

v_switch.description: The name of the switch to which the virtual entity is connected.

vlan_port_group.description: Port group which the virtual machine belongs to.

vmhost_ip_address.description: IP address of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_mac_address.description: MAC address of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_name.description: Name of the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted.

vmhost_nic_names.description: List of all physical port names used by the virtual switch on the physical node on which the virtual machine is hosted. Represented as: "eth1,eth2,eth3".

os.description: The operating system of the detected host or virtual entity. weight.description: The weight of the SRV record. Valid values are from 0 to 65535 (inclusive), in 32-bit unsigned integer format.

blacklist_action.description: The action to perform when a domain name matches the pattern defined in a rule that is specified by the blacklist_ruleset method. Valid values are "REDIRECT" or "REFUSE". The default value is "REDIRECT".

blacklist_redirect_addresses.description: The array of IP addresses the appliance includes in the response it sends in place of a blacklisted IP address.

blacklist_redirect_ttl.description: The Time To Live (TTL) value of the synthetic DNS responses resulted from blacklist redirection. The TTL value is a 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the TTL in seconds.

blacklist_rulesets.description: The name of the Ruleset object assigned at the Grid level for blacklist redirection.

You can either select and use Internet root name servers or specify custom root name servers by providing a host name and IP address to which the Infoblox appliance can send queries. Include the specified parameter to set the attribute value. Omit the parameter to retrieve the attribute value.
